name: Yacimiento de los Cayos — circular desde Cornago por GR 93
region_intro: Hiking around Cornago offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Alcarama, characterized by a dry climate and varied vegetation including pine repopulations, gorse, heather, and juniper. The village is set on a hillside, crowned by a castle and church, providing a scenic starting point for exploration. Prominent natural features include the limestone bastion of Peña Isasa and the Linares river, which flows through the area. This region presents a mix of mountainous terrain and riverine environments, suitable for various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Cornago The most popular hiking route is Peña Isasa — circular desde Muro de Aguas, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the prominent Peña Isasa. Another top favourite among local hikers is Despoblado de Vea by the río Linares – round trip from San Pedro Manrique, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. This trail follows the Linares river, offering riparian scenery. Local hikers also love the Yacimiento de los Cayos — circular desde Cornago por GR 93, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through historical sites and along the GR 93, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Cornago is defined by the mountainous terrain of Sierra de Alcarama, the presence of the Linares river, and diverse Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Cornago are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Cornago's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around Cornago?
A: Hiking around Cornago offers a diverse range of landscapes within the Sierra de Alcarama. You'll find mountainous terrain, riverine environments along the Linares river, and varied Mediterranean vegetation including pine forests, gorse, heather, and juniper. The trails cater to different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Cornago?
A: Yes, Cornago offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners. There are 3 easy routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207392574/chorron-del-saltadero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón del Saltadero Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is just 3.6 miles long and takes about 1 hour 43 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Cornago?
A: The region boasts several significant natural landmarks. You can hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;, a prominent limestone bastion reaching 1,474 meters, which offers impressive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062372964/despoblado-de-vea-by-the-rio-linares-round-trip-from-san-pedro-manrique" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despoblado de Vea by the río Linares – round trip from San Pedro Manrique&lt;/a&gt; trail follows the scenic Linares river, providing riparian views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Cornago?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207368877/pena-isasa-circular-desde-muro-de-aguas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa — circular desde Muro de Aguas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that takes about 3 hours 57 minutes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207340342/yacimiento-de-los-cayos-circular-desde-cornago-por-gr-93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de los Cayos — circular desde Cornago por GR 93&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.7 miles long and explores historical sites.

Q: What historical sites or cultural attractions can I explore near Cornago's hiking trails?
A: Cornago itself is crowned by an impressive castle and parish church, which are central to the village's identity. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celtiberian Archaeological Site of Contrebia Leucade&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a fascinating historical experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207340342/yacimiento-de-los-cayos-circular-desde-cornago-por-gr-93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de los Cayos — circular desde Cornago por GR 93&lt;/a&gt; trail specifically leads through historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cornago?
A: Given the region's dry climate, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in Cornago, offering pleasant temperatures. While summer can be warm, the diverse terrain provides options. Winter hiking is also possible, though conditions may vary.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Cornago?
A: There are 11 hiking trails available around Cornago, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes and 8 moderate routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cornago?
A: The hiking routes in Cornago are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to riverine environments, and the opportunity to explore historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of Cornago village itself?
A: Yes, some trails provide excellent vantage points of the village. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Cornago from the GR 93&lt;/a&gt; highlight indicates specific spots along the GR 93 where you can enjoy panoramic views of Cornago, with its castle and church perched on the hillside.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to summits or high points for panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;, a significant limestone peak. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242057510/pena-isasa-loop-from-palomares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa Loop from Palomares&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.8-mile trail that takes you up to this prominent summit, offering extensive views of the surrounding Sierra de Alcarama.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer waterside scenery?
A: Yes, the Linares river plays a significant role in the local scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062372964/despoblado-de-vea-by-the-rio-linares-round-trip-from-san-pedro-manrique" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despoblado de Vea by the río Linares – round trip from San Pedro Manrique&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.8-mile moderate path that specifically follows the Linares river, providing beautiful riparian views and a chance to experience the riverine environment.
tips:
About 300 inhabitants live in Cornago and it is framed within the Sierra de Alcarama.

The town stands out for its 18th century castle-fortress that stands imposingly on a hill and offers wonderful views of the town and the valley.

At the top of the Cornago hill is a beautiful 18th century castle, built during the Late Middle Ages. Rectangular in plan with a tower at each vertex, the castle was built in ashlar masonry on the ruins of an old fortress from the 9th century.
A town almost abandoned and half in ruins.
At the site we can see the footprints of dinosaurs, they lived at the beginning of the Lower Cretaceous, about 140 million years ago.
